reviews.review_msg: Olga is nice and helpful if you ask her for help. Lectures were very dull and boring aside from the weekly case discussions, which you HAVE to be HIGHLY ACTIVE in. Case readings were generally dull, but you do learn during discussion. Term project was fairly difficult. All about case analysis--just give adequate support for all your points.
She is nice and friendly. The course is really complicated. Still waiting for the grade. It has been a week since final.
Olga is knowledgeable and cares about her students, but isn't the best instructor out there. Has difficulty communicating her points across. She is, however, passionate about the subject.

It's hard to find another prof that cares so much about her students. A lot of the material she is lecturing on is, by it's nature, quite dry and hard to absorb in lectures. Read material for class beforehand and lectures will make sense. You'll find that the people here who made bad comments are simply bad students who came unprepared to lecture.

This teacher is terrible.  She is too stupid to mark exams and assignments properly. Do the smart thing and take it with Drew Parker. Olga is the worst prof. at SFU.  This class is useless, so at least take it with a prof who understands what he is teaching, rather than a moron like Olga.

I'm not sure why Olga has bad reviews on here. She's a great prof but I also love the subject. If you are an accounting student, you will find the material dry and uninteresting. She has good notes and provides good examples. The exams are tough, 54% midterms average and still waiting for final mark. Other than that, she is very approachable/friend
You better stop teaching, your're kinda useless compare to other MIS prof, and you're unfair, you will help student that you like.
Her voice not clear, demand a lot from student, very slow in marking, don't take with her.

She clearly knows her stuff and is adept at leading case discussions. You do have to prepare for discussions every week if you want to do well. The bulk of the course content seemed to not be necessary in the analysis of cases. Demanding marker, and takes a long time to return assignments and exams.

She has a lot of experience in MIS and knows what she is talking about. Besides the lectures, most of the class was case discussions. Make sure you participate a lot if you want to actually learn. Everything (including midterm) was case-based. No final. Hard marker, but nice curve.
Nice prof, very tough marker, lots of workload. Top notch cases and very interesting class discussions.

Her interest in the course is extremely high. She is the most enthusiastic prof ever. Unfortunately, her exams aren't what one would expect. Final was a shocker, a lot of the material was from before the mindterm and on the final! To do well, make sure to ask her questions and participate. She is extremely helpful. TA sucked. Book is good - theory.

Never again will I take this professor.  This is the first course that I've taken in the MIS concentration and it was downright lousy and horrible.  Very disorganized, exams took forever to mark and feedback was limited.  The exams are long and quite hard.  Textbook sucked.  This was a nightmare.
Worst prof ive had at sfu. didnt have out marks ready by the deadline. exams are long and painful. dont take this class with her.
Write your cases before the midterm. The midterm is worth more.

Great Prof. Need to really keep up with readings and PARTICIPATE. Write your cases after the midterm. You will get more of an example of what to write later and be able to write a better case than those who write them early.
